name: PocketSense — Consumer Pocket Monitor
approach: A compact, low-cost handheld optimized for everyday users: sensitive optical PM2.5 sensor, low-power NDIR CO2, combined temperature/humidity sensor, integrated display and smartphone/cloud sync. Meet the 8‑hour runtime with an energy-optimized parts selection and firmware power management.
highlights: Sensors: laser-scattering PM2.5 sensor (low-power optical module), low-power NDIR CO2 module, digital temperature + humidity (e.g., SHT-series)
Battery: internal 3000 mAh Li‑ion battery sized for 8 hours typical use (measurement every 10–30s, display on demand), USB‑C recharge
Connectivity: Bluetooth LE for continuous phone sync + Wi‑Fi for direct cloud upload when on a known network
UI: 1.3" OLED for real-time readings, simple single-button control and status LED; companion mobile app for graphing, alerts, firmware updates
Power management: sensor duty-cycling (PM and CO2 sampling cadence adjustable), display sleep, MCU deep-sleep between samples
Form factor: pocketable (≈120×55×25 mm), lightweight plastic housing with inlet port and ergonomic grip
Use case: consumer indoor/outdoor awareness, real-time alerts (air quality index), trip logging to app

name: ProFlow — Professional Active Sampler
approach: A rugged handheld instrument for more accurate field measurements: active air sampling with a micro-pump for stable PM2.5 measurements, higher-grade NDIR CO2 with automatic baseline routines, onboard data logging and robust wireless sync. Design prioritizes measurement accuracy and repeatability while still meeting an 8‑hour battery runtime.
highlights: Sensors & sampling: active micro-pump draws controlled flow (e.g., 0.5–1 L/min) into a high-precision optical PM2.5 sensor; high-accuracy NDIR CO2 (Senseair-class) and precision RH/temperature sensor
Battery: internal 5200 mAh Li‑ion battery engineered to power pump + sensors for 8 hours; fast USB‑C charging and removable battery option for extended deployments
Connectivity: dual-mode Wi‑Fi (802.11n) for bulk uploads and BLE for quick phone pairing and real-time monitoring
Data handling: onboard encrypted storage (microSD), configurable logging intervals (1s–5min), data export (CSV/JSON) and cloud integration
Calibration & QA: built-in auto-baseline/ABC routines for CO2, field calibration port for PM and CO2, replaceable sensor cartridges for lab recalibration
Ruggedization: rubberized, dust-resistant enclosure (IP54), instrument-graded mounting points and belt-clip; large backlit color display and tactile controls
Use case: professional indoor air assessments, building inspections, citizen science with higher accuracy requirements

name: EcoDuty — Ultra Low‑Power Field Monitor with E‑Ink
approach: A handheld designed around extreme power efficiency: e‑ink display + aggressive duty-cycling and wake-on-motion to guarantee ≥8 hours of realistic field operation while still providing PM2.5, CO2, temperature, humidity sensing and wireless sync. Ideal for long patrols and distributed monitoring where battery life and readability in sunlight matter.
highlights: Sensors: compact optical PM2.5 sensor optimized for short bursts, NDIR CO2 operated in intermittent sampling mode (e.g., 30s sample every 60–120s), high-efficiency temp/humidity sensor

Battery & power strategy: 3600 mAh Li‑ion battery + hardware power gating; e‑ink display (very low idle draw), motion sensor to keep device sleeping when idle — guaranteed 8+ hours under typical sampling profiles
Connectivity: BLE for frequent low-energy telemetry to phone; Wi‑Fi available for on-demand bulk sync when user initiates upload (reduces continuous Wi‑Fi drain)
UX: sunlight-readable e‑ink front display for persistent numeric + small trend graph, minimal buttons, optional haptic alert; mobile app for full history and cloud sync
Field options: optional solar trickle-charge panel on back for extending multi-day deployments, carabiner clip or strap holster
Firmware features: adaptive sampling (increases sampling cadence automatically when PM/CO2 exceed thresholds), OTA firmware updates via BLE/Wi‑Fi
Use case: long-duration field patrols, environmental surveys, school/park deployments where battery life and readability in daylight are priorities
